channel.title: flove making-of — standards questions
channel.description: New standards design questions from flove interviews.

item.title: What is the flove approach to mobile-first design?
item.description:
• Strict mobile-first — Design for mobile first, enhance for desktop. CSS media queries follow this pattern. • Desktop-first with mobile — Design for desktop, adapt for mobile. More common, less future-proof. • Equal targets — Design for both simultaneously. Balanced, more work.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Strict mobile-first

item.title: How should flove handle design system tokens?
item.description:
• Centralized tokens — Shared CSS variables across all apps. Consistent, less individual expression. • Per-app tokens — Each app defines its own variables. Flexible, less consistent. • Hybrid — Core tokens shared, apps can extend/override. Balanced.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Hybrid

item.title: Should flove apps have analytics?
item.description:
• No analytics — Keep it private. No tracking, no data. Matches §3.10.7. • Self-hosted (Matomo) — Full control, privacy-respecting, but requires hosting. • Privacy-first (Plausible) — Lightweight, GDPR-compliant, cloud-hosted. Easy but external. • Basic counters only — Simple hit counters per page. Minimal, self-contained.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: No analytics

item.title: What is the flove approach to error handling?
item.description:
• Graceful degradation — App continues working with reduced functionality. Best UX. • Error page — Show a friendly error message. Clear, but interrupts flow. • Silent failure — Fail silently, log to console. Minimal user impact, hard to debug. • Inline error states — Show error within the relevant UI component. Contextual, non-disruptive.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Graceful degradation

item.title: What is the flove approach to accessibility testing?
item.description:
• Automated + manual — Run axe/Lighthouse + manual screen reader testing. Most thorough. • Automated only — Just axe/Lighthouse. Catches obvious issues, misses nuance. • Manual only — Just manual testing. Thorough but slow, easy to skip. • AAA by design — Follow AAA patterns in development, test periodically. Proactive.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: AAA by design

item.title: Should flove apps have a consistent onboarding flow?
item.description:
• Standardized onboarding — All apps share a welcome/tour component. Consistent, more code. • Per-app onboarding — Each app designs its own. Flexible, potentially inconsistent. • No onboarding — Apps are self-explanatory. Simplest, assumes user familiarity. • Contextual hints — First-use tooltips and hints within the app. Non-disruptive, lightweight.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Contextual hints

item.title: How should flove handle app state persistence?
item.description:
• Full session restore — All state persists — user returns exactly where they left off. Heavy, private. • Preferences only — Just settings and preferences persist. Light, useful. • Nothing (ephemeral) — No persistence — fresh start each time. Most private, least convenient. • User choice — Let user decide what persists per app. Flexible, more UI.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Preferences only
